topics.description: Choose one revision target for a paragraph, such as order, detail, connector, word choice, or ending sentence.
evidence: Name one revision target before revising.
Make a revision that clearly matches the target.
topics.assessmentPrompt: Ask the student to choose one paragraph revision target and show the before/after change.

topics.subject: Japanese
topics.name: Proofreading romaji input into kana
topics.japaneseName: ローマ字入力後のかな確認
topics.description: Proofread kana created by romaji input for small-tsu, long vowels, particle spellings, and conversion boundaries.
evidence: Find one kana error after romaji input.
Explain whether the issue is sound, spelling, or conversion boundary.
topics.assessmentPrompt: Give a short typed sentence and ask the student to proofread kana input errors.

topics.subject: Japanese
topics.name: Switching reading strategy note
topics.japaneseName: 読み方を切り替えたメモ
topics.description: Notice when one reading strategy is not working and switch to rereading, dictionary lookup, diagram use, or asking a question.
evidence: Name the first strategy used and why it was not enough.
Choose a second strategy and record whether it helped.
topics.assessmentPrompt: Ask the student to keep a strategy-switch note during informational reading.

topics.subject: Japanese
topics.name: First reliability check for a source
topics.japaneseName: 情報源のはじめの信頼性チェック
topics.description: Make an age-appropriate first reliability check using title, author, date, source type, and purpose.
evidence: Identify title, author or publisher, date, and source type.
State one reason the source is useful or needs checking.
topics.assessmentPrompt: Give two sources on one topic and ask the student to make a first reliability check.

topics.subject: Japanese
topics.name: Evidence tags in a summary draft
topics.japaneseName: 要約下書きの根拠タグ
topics.description: Tag which source detail supports each sentence in a summary draft to avoid unsupported or copied summary writing.
evidence: Mark the source detail behind each summary sentence.
Revise one unsupported or too-close sentence.
topics.assessmentPrompt: Ask the student to tag a summary draft with source evidence and revise one sentence.

topics.subject: Japanese
topics.name: Synthesizing visual and text in one sentence
topics.japaneseName: 図と本文を一文でまとめる
topics.description: Combine information from a visual, caption, and prose sentence into one accurate synthesis sentence.
evidence: Select one visual detail and one text detail about the same point.
Write a synthesis sentence that uses both details accurately.
topics.assessmentPrompt: Ask the student to write one sentence using both a diagram/caption and the prose explanation.

topics.subject: Japanese
topics.name: Theme tracker across chapters
topics.japaneseName: 章をまたいだ主題トラッカー
topics.description: Track how repeated character choices, images, or conflicts build a theme across more than one scene or chapter.
evidence: Record repeated evidence from at least two parts of a story.
Explain how the repeated evidence supports a possible theme.
topics.assessmentPrompt: Ask the student to keep a theme tracker while reading a multi-scene story.

topics.subject: Japanese
topics.name: Facilitator return question
topics.japaneseName: 司会が話を戻す質問
topics.description: Use a facilitator question to return discussion to text evidence, topic focus, or an unresolved question.
evidence: Notice when discussion has drifted or needs evidence.
Ask a return question that brings the group back to the task.
topics.assessmentPrompt: During group discussion, ask the student facilitator to use one return question.

topics.subject: Japanese
topics.name: Audience hook in revision
topics.japaneseName: 読み手を意識した書き出しの推敲
topics.description: Revise the opening of an explanation, report, or opinion so the reader understands the topic and wants to continue.
evidence: Identify whether the opening names topic, purpose, or reader interest.
Revise the opening to better orient the reader.
topics.assessmentPrompt: Ask the student to revise the opening of a report or opinion for a specific reader.

topics.subject: Japanese
topics.name: Dialect and register comparison note
topics.japaneseName: 方言と文体の比較メモ
topics.description: Compare dialect, standard language, polite style, and casual style as different kinds of language variation.
evidence: Sort examples as dialect, standard language, polite style, or casual style.
Explain when or why one example might be used.
topics.assessmentPrompt: Ask the student to compare language examples and write a short use-context note.

topics.subject: Japanese
topics.name: Metaphoric meaning in kanji compounds
topics.japaneseName: 熟語に見られる比喩的な意味
topics.description: Notice when a kanji compound has a meaning that extends beyond the literal meanings of its parts.
evidence: Identify component meanings in a familiar compound.
Explain how the compound meaning extends or shifts from the parts.
topics.assessmentPrompt: Ask the student to explain a familiar compound whose meaning is not purely literal.

topics.subject: Japanese
topics.name: Proofing homophone word choice by style and meaning
topics.japaneseName: 同音語を意味と文体で見直す
topics.description: Check same-sound word choices by sentence meaning, kanji, genre, and reader expectation.
evidence: Identify a same-sound word choice that could confuse meaning.
Justify the chosen word with context and reader fit.
topics.assessmentPrompt: Give sentences with homophone choices and ask the student to justify the correct word.

topics.subject: Japanese
topics.name: Turning listening notes into a summary
topics.japaneseName: 聞き取りメモから要約する
topics.description: Turn key-word listening notes into a short oral or written summary with main point and supporting detail.
evidence: Select the main point from listening notes.
Produce a short summary using one supporting detail.
topics.assessmentPrompt: After a short explanation, ask the student to make notes and summarize from them.

topics.subject: Japanese
topics.name: Peer revision conference stems
topics.japaneseName: 推敲会議の話し方
topics.description: Use respectful sentence stems to ask about clarity, evidence, order, or word choice in a peer writing conference.
evidence: Ask a revision question using respectful wording.
Connect the question to a specific part of the peer draft.
topics.assessmentPrompt: During peer revision, ask the student to use two conference stems tied to draft details.

topics.subject: Japanese
topics.name: Reflecting on a source gap
topics.japaneseName: 情報の足りないところを振り返る
topics.description: Reflect on what information is still missing after reading sources and choose a next source or question.
evidence: Name one information gap in current notes.
Choose a next source, question, or lookup route to address the gap.
topics.assessmentPrompt: Ask the student to review source notes and write one gap plus a next search action.

topics.subject: Japanese
topics.name: Combining sentences for flow
topics.japaneseName: 文をつないで流れをよくする
topics.description: Combine or reorder short sentences so the relationship between cause, example, and explanation is clearer.
evidence: Identify two short sentences that should be connected or reordered.
Revise them with a connector, modifier, or order change that improves flow.
topics.assessmentPrompt: Give choppy explanatory sentences and ask the student to combine or reorder them for flow.

topics.subject: Japanese
topics.name: Grade 1 audience-aware retell checkpoint
topics.japaneseName: G1聞き手に合わせた再話チェック
topics.description: Audience-aware retell checkpoint means retelling so the listener gets the needed event order, emphasis, and omitted background rather than a memorized plot dump. At grade 1, the learner applies it in short familiar classroom talk, reading, or writing by producing a sentence, short note, retell, copied passage, or oral response with teacher support and explaining one visible choice in simple language.
evidence: Identify the relevant language, text, or source feature in short familiar classroom talk, reading, or writing.
Produce or revise a sentence, short note, retell, copied passage, or oral response with teacher support that shows audience-aware retell checkpoint.
Explain one visible choice in simple language.
topics.assessmentPrompt: Give the student a grade 1 task involving audience-aware retell checkpoint; ask them to produce or revise a response and explain one evidence-based choice.

topics.subject: Japanese
topics.name: Grade 1 listening-note question lift
topics.japaneseName: G1聞き取りメモから質問を立てる
topics.description: Listening-note question lift means turning listening notes into questions by separating heard facts, uncertain points, and useful follow-up prompts. At grade 1, the learner applies it in short familiar classroom talk, reading, or writing by producing a sentence, short note, retell, copied passage, or oral response with teacher support and explaining one visible choice in simple language.
evidence: Identify the relevant language, text, or source feature in short familiar classroom talk, reading, or writing.
Produce or revise a sentence, short note, retell, copied passage, or oral response with teacher support that shows listening-note question lift.
Explain one visible choice in simple language.
topics.assessmentPrompt: Give the student a grade 1 task involving listening-note question lift; ask them to produce or revise a response and explain one evidence-based choice.

topics.subject: Japanese
topics.name: Grade 1 interview follow-up cue
topics.japaneseName: G1インタビューの追質問キュー
topics.description: Interview follow-up cue means keeping an interview moving with follow-up questions that connect to the answer, clarify detail, and maintain respect. At grade 1, the learner applies it in short familiar classroom talk, reading, or writing by producing a sentence, short note, retell, copied passage, or oral response with teacher support and explaining one visible choice in simple language.
evidence: Identify the relevant language, text, or source feature in short familiar classroom talk, reading, or writing.
Produce or revise a sentence, short note, retell, copied passage, or oral response with teacher support that shows interview follow-up cue.
Explain one visible choice in simple language.
topics.assessmentPrompt: Give the student a grade 1 task involving interview follow-up cue; ask them to produce or revise a response and explain one evidence-based choice.

topics.subject: Japanese
topics.name: Grade 1 discussion turn-building move
topics.japaneseName: G1話し合いの発言接続
topics.description: Discussion turn-building move means linking a new contribution to what someone else said by adding evidence, asking a question, or extending the idea. At grade 1, the learner applies it in short familiar classroom talk, reading, or writing by producing a sentence, short note, retell, copied passage, or oral response with teacher support and explaining one visible choice in simple language.
evidence: Identify the relevant language, text, or source feature in short familiar classroom talk, reading, or writing.
Produce or revise a sentence, short note, retell, copied passage, or oral response with teacher support that shows discussion turn-building move.
Explain one visible choice in simple language.
topics.assessmentPrompt: Give the student a grade 1 task involving discussion turn-building move; ask them to produce or revise a response and explain one evidence-based choice.

topics.subject: Japanese
topics.name: Grade 1 presentation structure signal
topics.japaneseName: G1発表構成の合図
topics.description: Presentation structure signal means using spoken or written signals that mark the opening, order, emphasis, transition, and closing of a presentation. At grade 1, the learner applies it in short familiar classroom talk, reading, or writing by producing a sentence, short note, retell, copied passage, or oral response with teacher support and explaining one visible choice in simple language.
evidence: Identify the relevant language, text, or source feature in short familiar classroom talk, reading, or writing.
Produce or revise a sentence, short note, retell, copied passage, or oral response with teacher support that shows presentation structure signal.
Explain one visible choice in simple language.
topics.assessmentPrompt: Give the student a grade 1 task involving presentation structure signal; ask them to produce or revise a response and explain one evidence-based choice.

topics.subject: Japanese
topics.name: Grade 1 debate response evidence check
topics.japaneseName: G1討論応答の根拠確認
topics.description: Debate response evidence check means checking whether a debate response answers the claim, uses evidence, explains the warrant, and addresses a counterpoint. At grade 1, the learner applies it in short familiar classroom talk, reading, or writing by producing a sentence, short note, retell, copied passage, or oral response with teacher support and explaining one visible choice in simple language.
evidence: Identify the relevant language, text, or source feature in short familiar classroom talk, reading, or writing.
Produce or revise a sentence, short note, retell, copied passage, or oral response with teacher support that shows debate response evidence check.
Explain one visible choice in simple language.
topics.assessmentPrompt: Give the student a grade 1 task involving debate response evidence check; ask them to produce or revise a response and explain one evidence-based choice.

topics.subject: Japanese
topics.name: Grade 1 kana-kanji proofreading pass
topics.japaneseName: G1かな漢字の見直しパス
topics.description: Kana-kanji proofreading pass means rereading for kana, kanji conversion, okurigana, punctuation, and reader-facing notation errors. At grade 1, the learner applies it in short familiar classroom talk, reading, or writing by producing a sentence, short note, retell, copied passage, or oral response with teacher support and explaining one visible choice in simple language.
evidence: Identify the relevant language, text, or source feature in short familiar classroom talk, reading, or writing.
Produce or revise a sentence, short note, retell, copied passage, or oral response with teacher support that shows kana-kanji proofreading pass.
Explain one visible choice in simple language.
topics.assessmentPrompt: Give the student a grade 1 task involving kana-kanji proofreading pass; ask them to produce or revise a response and explain one evidence-based choice.

topics.subject: Japanese
topics.name: Grade 1 digital text notation control
topics.japaneseName: G1デジタル文章表記の調整
topics.description: Digital text notation control means controlling Japanese digital text conventions such as conversion choices, punctuation, line breaks, spacing, and readability. At grade 1, the learner applies it in short familiar classroom talk, reading, or writing by producing a sentence, short note, retell, copied passage, or oral response with teacher support and explaining one visible choice in simple language.
evidence: Identify the relevant language, text, or source feature in short familiar classroom talk, reading, or writing.
Produce or revise a sentence, short note, retell, copied passage, or oral response with teacher support that shows digital text notation control.
Explain one visible choice in simple language.
topics.assessmentPrompt: Give the student a grade 1 task involving digital text notation control; ask them to produce or revise a response and explain one evidence-based choice.

topics.subject: Japanese
topics.name: Grade 1 kanji component-to-meaning explanation
topics.japaneseName: G1漢字構成から意味を説明する
topics.description: Kanji component-to-meaning explanation means using components, radicals, semantic families, and reading clues to explain how a kanji or compound suggests meaning. At grade 1, the learner applies it in short familiar classroom talk, reading, or writing by producing a sentence, short note, retell, copied passage, or oral response with teacher support and explaining one visible choice in simple language.
evidence: Identify the relevant language, text, or source feature in short familiar classroom talk, reading, or writing.
Produce or revise a sentence, short note, retell, copied passage, or oral response with teacher support that shows kanji component-to-meaning explanation.
Explain one visible choice in simple language.
topics.assessmentPrompt: Give the student a grade 1 task involving kanji component-to-meaning explanation; ask them to produce or revise a response and explain one evidence-based choice.

topics.subject: Japanese
topics.name: Grade 1 kanji compound domain transfer
topics.japaneseName: G1熟語を分野へ転用する
topics.description: Kanji compound domain transfer means using a familiar kanji compound pattern to understand or produce vocabulary in a new subject domain. At grade 1, the learner applies it in short familiar classroom talk, reading, or writing by producing a sentence, short note, retell, copied passage, or oral response with teacher support and explaining one visible choice in simple language.
evidence: Identify the relevant language, text, or source feature in short familiar classroom talk, reading, or writing.
Produce or revise a sentence, short note, retell, copied passage, or oral response with teacher support that shows kanji compound domain transfer.
Explain one visible choice in simple language.
topics.assessmentPrompt: Give the student a grade 1 task involving kanji compound domain transfer; ask them to produce or revise a response and explain one evidence-based choice.

topics.subject: Japanese
topics.name: Grade 1 okurigana reading decision
topics.japaneseName: G1送り仮名による読みの判断
topics.description: Okurigana reading decision means using okurigana to recover word boundaries, inflection, dictionary forms, and likely readings. At grade 1, the learner applies it in short familiar classroom talk, reading, or writing by producing a sentence, short note, retell, copied passage, or oral response with teacher support and explaining one visible choice in simple language.
evidence: Identify the relevant language, text, or source feature in short familiar classroom talk, reading, or writing.
Produce or revise a sentence, short note, retell, copied passage, or oral response with teacher support that shows okurigana reading decision.
Explain one visible choice in simple language.
topics.assessmentPrompt: Give the student a grade 1 task involving okurigana reading decision; ask them to produce or revise a response and explain one evidence-based choice.

topics.subject: Japanese
topics.name: Grade 1 context-based word-sense choice
topics.japaneseName: G1文脈に合う語義選択
topics.description: Context-based word-sense choice means choosing the sense of a word from sentence context, collocation, topic, and surrounding explanation. At grade 1, the learner applies it in short familiar classroom talk, reading, or writing by producing a sentence, short note, retell, copied passage, or oral response with teacher support and explaining one visible choice in simple language.
evidence: Identify the relevant language, text, or source feature in short familiar classroom talk, reading, or writing.
Produce or revise a sentence, short note, retell, copied passage, or oral response with teacher support that shows context-based word-sense choice.
Explain one visible choice in simple language.
topics.assessmentPrompt: Give the student a grade 1 task involving context-based word-sense choice; ask them to produce or revise a response and explain one evidence-based choice.

topics.subject: Japanese
topics.name: Grade 1 register-sensitive word choice
topics.japaneseName: G1場面に応じた語の選択
topics.description: Register-sensitive word choice means choosing words that fit relationship, formality, stance, medium, and reader expectation. At grade 1, the learner applies it in short familiar classroom talk, reading, or writing by producing a sentence, short note, retell, copied passage, or oral response with teacher support and explaining one visible choice in simple language.
evidence: Identify the relevant language, text, or source feature in short familiar classroom talk, reading, or writing.
Produce or revise a sentence, short note, retell, copied passage, or oral response with teacher support that shows register-sensitive word choice.
Explain one visible choice in simple language.
topics.assessmentPrompt: Give the student a grade 1 task involving register-sensitive word choice; ask them to produce or revise a response and explain one evidence-based choice.

topics.subject: Japanese
topics.name: Grade 1 technical term meaning note
topics.japaneseName: G1専門語の意味メモ
topics.description: Technical term meaning note means making a usable note for a technical term with definition, example, source context, related words, and domain limits. At grade 1, the learner applies it in short familiar classroom talk, reading, or writing by producing a sentence, short note, retell, copied passage, or oral response with teacher support and explaining one visible choice in simple language.
evidence: Identify the relevant language, text, or source feature in short familiar classroom talk, reading, or writing.
Produce or revise a sentence, short note, retell, copied passage, or oral response with teacher support that shows technical term meaning note.
Explain one visible choice in simple language.
topics.assessmentPrompt: Give the student a grade 1 task involving technical term meaning note; ask them to produce or revise a response and explain one evidence-based choice.

topics.subject: Japanese
topics.name: Grade 1 sentence connector function label
topics.japaneseName: G1接続表現の働きを名付ける
topics.description: Sentence connector function label means labeling what a connector does in a sentence or paragraph, such as sequence, cause, contrast, condition, or addition. At grade 1, the learner applies it in short familiar classroom talk, reading, or writing by producing a sentence, short note, retell, copied passage, or oral response with teacher support and explaining one visible choice in simple language.
evidence: Identify the relevant language, text, or source feature in short familiar classroom talk, reading, or writing.
Produce or revise a sentence, short note, retell, copied passage, or oral response with teacher support that shows sentence connector function label.
Explain one visible choice in simple language.
topics.assessmentPrompt: Give the student a grade 1 task involving sentence connector function label; ask them to produce or revise a response and explain one evidence-based choice.

topics.subject: Japanese
topics.name: Grade 1 clause scope editing move
topics.japaneseName: G1節のかかり受け編集
topics.description: Clause scope editing move means finding which words a modifier or clause controls, then revising boundaries to remove ambiguity. At grade 1, the learner applies it in short familiar classroom talk, reading, or writing by producing a sentence, short note, retell, copied passage, or oral response with teacher support and explaining one visible choice in simple language.
evidence: Identify the relevant language, text, or source feature in short familiar classroom talk, reading, or writing.
Produce or revise a sentence, short note, retell, copied passage, or oral response with teacher support that shows clause scope editing move.
Explain one visible choice in simple language.
topics.assessmentPrompt: Give the student a grade 1 task involving clause scope editing move; ask them to produce or revise a response and explain one evidence-based choice.

topics.subject: Japanese
topics.name: Grade 1 style-ending control
topics.japaneseName: G1文末表現の統一と調整
topics.description: Style-ending control means choosing and maintaining sentence-final style, including desu/masu, da/de aru, plain endings, quoted speech, literary endings, and explanatory endings. At grade 1, the learner recognizes whether classroom speech or a short written sentence sounds polite or casual, then keeps a simple response in the same style unless it is quoted speech.
evidence: Identify the ending style in a sentence or short passage, such as desu/masu, da/de aru, plain narration, quoted speech, or explanatory style.
Revise a grade 1 response so sentence endings fit the audience and genre without accidental style mixing.
Explain one ending choice in terms of reader, speaker relationship, quoted voice, or text type.
topics.assessmentPrompt: Give the student a grade 1 draft with mixed sentence endings and ask them to mark the style, revise the mismatches, and explain one ending choice.

topics.subject: Japanese
topics.name: Grade 1 character motive inference
topics.japaneseName: G1人物の動機を推論する
topics.description: Character motive inference means inferring why a character acts by connecting actions, speech, description, and textual evidence. At grade 1, the learner applies it in short familiar classroom talk, reading, or writing by producing a sentence, short note, retell, copied passage, or oral response with teacher support and explaining one visible choice in simple language.
evidence: Identify the relevant language, text, or source feature in short familiar classroom talk, reading, or writing.
Produce or revise a sentence, short note, retell, copied passage, or oral response with teacher support that shows character motive inference.
Explain one visible choice in simple language.
topics.assessmentPrompt: Give the student a grade 1 task involving character motive inference; ask them to produce or revise a response and explain one evidence-based choice.

topics.subject: Japanese
topics.name: Grade 1 theme and image pattern trace
topics.japaneseName: G1主題とイメージの反復を追う
topics.description: Theme and image pattern trace means tracking repeated images, motifs, tone shifts, and wording patterns to explain theme development.
